text:
If you’re watching gymnastics at theOlympics, you can’t help but notice that every competitor is using chalk.

For all gymnasts competing on bars, rings, pommel horse and even the balance beam, chalk is an essential part of the preparation moments before a routine.

Chalk is undoubtedly messy to use, some commercial gyms have even banned it due to the extensive clean up required.

But for elite gymnasts, chalk will remain a vital tool in the sport.

Chalk is vital for gymnasts to ensure that they can maintain their strong grip.

The chalk helps absorb moisture on the hands and fingers, which eradicates the problem of sweaty palms and gymnasts losing their grip on the bar.

It also helps decrease friction which allows gymnasts to have a smooth spin during rotations.

Female gymnasts also apply chalk to their feet before a routine on the balance beam.

Male gymnasts also apply chalk on their armpits for the parallel bars as they swing with their upper arms.

Without chalk, there would be a drastic rise in falls.

Gymnastics chalk is made from Magnesium Carbonate (MgCO3). It can typically be purchased as a powder, in a block or in liquid form, although that is usually more expensive.

It is different to the chalk used in used in classrooms, which is made from Calcium Sulphate (CaSO4) and doesn’t absorb moisture in the same way.

title: Why do gymnasts use chalk at the Olympics? | Metro News
description: Find out why gymnasts put chalk on their hands before the start of their routines at the Paris 2024 Olympics.
